25 Glen Shian Lane · Mt Eliza

A home already resolved. In a place that asks how you want your days to feel.

Two large north-facing blocks on Mt Eliza's Golden Mile. Designed, documented and approved, with a fixed-price pathway to build.

Canadian Bay
Land & Location

Large Golden Mile land, designed and approved, ready to build.

Two large blocks on Mt Eliza's Golden Mile, 1,835m² and 1,450m². Generous, established sites of a scale that rarely comes to market.

Rarer still with the work already done. The design is resolved, the documentation is complete and planning has been approved. What usually takes years of land, design and approvals is finished, leaving a clear, fixed-price path to build.

While each are distinctive, both homes are oriented to the north along their length. That aspect carries light into the courtyards, the pool decks and the main living areas through the year, the kind of natural warmth that is difficult to design in after the fact. Glen Shian Lane itself runs as a wide, established boulevard, a short walk from the village, the beach and the area's leading schools.

On Your Doorstep

The village, the bay and the schools, all within a short walk.

Straight-line distances from 25 Glen Shian Lane.

The Place

Most people drive straight past Mt Eliza. That's part of its appeal.

On summer weekends the Peninsula fills with people heading further south, to Mornington, Sorrento and Portsea. Mt Eliza rests quietly outside the influx of visitors. You can still find a park in the village, still get a table, still walk onto a beach and find almost nobody there but the locals. They tend to prefer it that way.

It is a genuine village. Familiar faces at the butcher, familiar parents at Saturday sport, children walking to school with friends. There are extraordinary homes here, many concealed behind established foliage. People come for space, privacy and permanence, not for visibility.

Resolved & Ready

The hard part is done. The build is ready to move.

Both homes are fully designed, documented and specified, down to the bespoke fittings, finishes and interiors. There is nothing left to resolve before construction, which removes the time, cost and risk a new build usually carries at the front end.

Site clearing and demolition are scheduled to begin in July 2026. From contract, construction can commence in roughly two months, with completion around twelve months after that.

Now
Contract and personal selections, while the window is open.
July 2026
Site clearing and demolition begin. This is happening.
~2 months
Construction commences after contract.
~12 months
Completion and handover of a finished home.

There is still room to make the selections, finishes and fixtures your own. That window narrows as works begin, so the earlier you explore this opportunity, the more of the home is yours to shape.

The Residences

Two distinct homes, resolved to the same uncompromising standard.

Turnkey & Complete

One builder. One contract. A complete custom designed home.

The price covers the whole outcome, not just the house. Pool, fencing, landscaping, driveway and external works are all designed and delivered by Latitude 37. There are no separate trades to coordinate and no gaps to manage between contracts.

Tiled concrete pool Fencing Designed & built landscaping Driveway External works Bespoke interiors throughout
The Builder

Built by Latitude 37.

Latitude 37 has been designing and building bespoke homes since 2006, with a long record of award-winning work across Melbourne and the Mornington Peninsula, including Mt Eliza itself. Design, interiors and construction sit within one team. That is how a project of this kind stays resolved from the first plan to handover.
